---
title: "About Matteo Arellano"
description: "The personal story behind Matteo's Lab, from Morelos to Rotterdam and the communities that shaped how Matteo approaches research, technology and service."
author: "Matteo Arellano"
lang: en
image: "images/home/matteo-in-guatemala.jpeg"
image-alt: "Matteo Arellano travelling by boat with a community group on a river in Guatemala."
page-layout: full
toc: false
title-block-style: none

open-graph:
  title: "About Matteo Arellano | Matteo's Lab"
  description: "The story behind Matteo's Lab and the experiences that shaped its purpose."
  image: "https://lab.matteoarellano.com/images/social/matteos-lab-social-card.png"

twitter-card:
  card-style: summary_large_image
  title: "About Matteo Arellano | Matteo's Lab"
  description: "The story behind Matteo's Lab and the experiences that shaped its purpose."
  image: "https://lab.matteoarellano.com/images/social/matteos-lab-social-card.png"

format:
  html:
    include-in-header:
      - includes/about-schema.html
---

<style>
  #title-block-header {
    display: none !important;
  }

  #quarto-content,
  main.content {
    max-width: none !important;
    margin: 0 !important;
    padding: 0 !important;
  }

  .page-columns {
    display: block !important;
  }

  :root {
    --story-forest: #214f3b;
    --story-leaf: #3f7d55;
    --story-green: #66a36f;
    --story-mint: #dcefe1;
    --story-light: #f2f8f1;
    --story-sand: #f3ead7;
    --story-clay: #b76544;
    --story-river: #45a4a0;
    --story-ink: #24382e;
    --story-muted: #5c6f64;
    --story-white: #ffffff;
    --story-border: rgba(33, 79, 59, 0.16);
  }

  .about-story-page {
    overflow: hidden;
    color: var(--story-ink);
    background: #fffefa;
  }

  .about-story-shell {
    width: min(1080px, calc(100% - 2rem));
    margin: 0 auto;
  }

  /* HERO */

  .about-story-hero {
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
    padding: 5.5rem 0;
    background:
      radial-gradient(
        circle at 88% 12%,
        rgba(69, 164, 160, 0.16),
        transparent 30%
      ),
      linear-gradient(
        135deg,
        #edf7ec 0%,
        #dcefe1 55%,
        #f3ead7 100%
      );
  }

  .about-story-hero::before {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    inset: 0;
    opacity: 0.07;
    pointer-events: none;
    background-image:
      linear-gradient(
        135deg,
        transparent 42%,
        var(--story-forest) 43% 47%,
        transparent 48%
      ),
      linear-gradient(
        45deg,
        transparent 42%,
        var(--story-leaf) 43% 47%,
        transparent 48%
      );
    background-size: 42px 42px;
    mask-image: linear-gradient(to left, black, transparent 75%);
  }

  .about-story-hero__inner {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 2;
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: minmax(0, 1fr) minmax(330px, 0.72fr);
    gap: 4rem;
    align-items: center;
  }

  .about-story-kicker {
    margin: 0 0 1rem;
    color: var(--story-leaf);
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    font-weight: 800;
    letter-spacing: 0.17em;
    text-transform: uppercase;
  }

  .about-story-hero h1 {
    max-width: 720px;
    margin: 0;
    padding: 0;
    border: 0 !important;
    color: var(--story-forest);
    font-size: clamp(3rem, 5.6vw, 5.8rem);
    line-height: 1.02;
    letter-spacing: -0.05em;
  }

  .about-story-lead {
    max-width: 710px;
    margin: 2rem 0 0;
    color: #405b4c;
    font-size: clamp(1.15rem, 1.8vw, 1.35rem);
    line-height: 1.8;
  }

  .about-story-photo {
    margin: 0;
    overflow: hidden;
    border: 10px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.72);
    border-radius: 28px;
    background: var(--story-white);
    box-shadow: 0 24px 60px rgba(33, 79, 59, 0.16);
    transform: rotate(1.5deg);
  }

  .about-story-photo img {
    width: 100%;
    height: 520px;
    display: block;
    object-fit: cover;
    object-position: center;
  }

  .about-story-photo figcaption {
    padding: 1rem 1.15rem 1.1rem;
    color: var(--story-muted);
    font-size: 0.9rem;
    line-height: 1.55;
    background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.95);
  }

  /* STORY SECTIONS */

  .about-story-section {
    padding: 5.5rem 0;
  }

  .about-story-section--green {
    background: var(--story-light);
  }

  .about-story-section--sand {
    background: #faf5e9;
  }

  .about-story-section--white {
    background: var(--story-white);
  }

  .about-story-narrow {
    max-width: 790px;
  }

  .about-story-section h2 {
    margin: 0 0 1.8rem;
    padding: 0;
    border: 0 !important;
    color: var(--story-forest);
    font-size: clamp(2rem, 3.7vw, 3.4rem);
    line-height: 1.12;
    letter-spacing: -0.035em;
  }

  .about-story-section p {
    margin: 0 0 1.55rem;
    color: var(--story-ink);
    font-size: 1.1rem;
    line-height: 1.95;
  }

  .about-story-section p:last-child {
    margin-bottom: 0;
  }

  .about-story-opening {
    color: var(--story-forest) !important;
    font-size: 1.3rem !important;
    line-height: 1.85 !important;
  }

  /* FAMILY */

  .about-family-grid {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: repeat(2, minmax(0, 1fr));
    gap: 1.3rem;
    margin-top: 3rem;
  }

  .about-family-card {
    position: relative;
    padding: 2.2rem;
    overflow: hidden;
    border: 1px solid var(--story-border);
    border-radius: 24px;
    background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
  }

  .about-family-card::after {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    right: -3.5rem;
    bottom: -3.5rem;
    width: 9rem;
    height: 9rem;
    border: 1px solid rgba(63, 125, 85, 0.11);
    border-radius: 50%;
    box-shadow:
      0 0 0 25px rgba(63, 125, 85, 0.025),
      0 0 0 50px rgba(63, 125, 85, 0.015);
  }

  .about-family-card__label {
    display: block;
    margin-bottom: 1.5rem;
    color: var(--story-clay);
    font-size: 0.77rem;
    font-weight: 800;
    letter-spacing: 0.14em;
  }

  .about-family-card h3 {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 2;
    margin: 0 0 1rem;
    color: var(--story-forest);
    font-size: 1.45rem;
  }

  .about-family-card p {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 2;
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--story-muted);
    font-size: 1rem;
    line-height: 1.8;
  }

  /* QUOTE */

  .about-story-quote {
    position: relative;
    margin: 3rem 0;
    padding: 2rem 2.2rem;
    border: 0;
    border-left: 5px solid var(--story-river);
    border-radius: 0 22px 22px 0;
    color: var(--story-forest);
    background: rgba(69, 164, 160, 0.1);
    font-size: clamp(1.25rem, 2.2vw, 1.65rem);
    font-weight: 650;
    line-height: 1.65;
  }

  .about-story-quote-light {
    position: relative;
    margin: 3rem 0;
    padding: 2rem 2.2rem;
    border: 0;
    border-left: 5px solid var(--story-river);
    border-radius: 0 22px 22px 0;
    color: var(--story-mint);
    background: rgba(69, 164, 160, 0.1);
    font-size: clamp(1.25rem, 2.2vw, 1.65rem);
    font-weight: 650;
    line-height: 1.65;
  }

  /* JOURNEY */

  .about-places {
    display: grid;
    gap: 1.2rem;
    margin-top: 3rem;
  }

  .about-place {
    display: grid;
    grid-template-columns: 125px 1fr;
    gap: 2rem;
    padding: 2rem 0;
    border-top: 1px solid var(--story-border);
  }

  .about-place:last-child {
    border-bottom: 1px solid var(--story-border);
  }

  .about-place__name {
    color: var(--story-leaf);
    font-size: 0.8rem;
    font-weight: 850;
    letter-spacing: 0.13em;
  }

  .about-place h3 {
    margin: 0 0 0.8rem;
    color: var(--story-forest);
    font-size: 1.4rem;
  }

  .about-place p {
    margin: 0;
    color: var(--story-muted);
    font-size: 1.02rem;
    line-height: 1.85;
  }

  /* FINAL SECTION */

  .about-story-final {
    position: relative;
    overflow: hidden;
    padding: 6rem 0;
    color: var(--story-white);
    background:
      radial-gradient(
        circle at 90% 20%,
        rgba(102, 163, 111, 0.38),
        transparent 32%
      ),
      linear-gradient(
        135deg,
        #214f3b,
        #316a4b 58%,
        #397d69
      );
  }

  .about-story-final::after {
    content: "";
    position: absolute;
    right: -8rem;
    bottom: -10rem;
    width: 28rem;
    height: 28rem;
    border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12);
    border-radius: 50%;
    box-shadow:
      0 0 0 55px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.025),
      0 0 0 110px rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.014);
  }

  .about-story-final__inner {
    position: relative;
    z-index: 2;
    max-width: 820px;
  }

  .about-story-final h2 {
    margin: 0 0 1.8rem;
    padding: 0;
    border: 0 !important;
    color: var(--story-white);
    font-size: clamp(2.5rem, 4.5vw, 4.5rem);
    line-height: 1.05;
    letter-spacing: -0.045em;
  }

  .about-story-final p {
    margin: 0 0 1.5rem;
    color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
    font-size: 1.12rem;
    line-height: 1.9;
  }

  .about-story-final strong {
    color: #dcf6df;
  }

  @media (max-width: 900px) {
    .about-story-hero__inner {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }

    .about-story-photo {
      max-width: 580px;
      transform: none;
    }

    .about-family-grid {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
    }
  }

  @media (max-width: 620px) {
    .about-story-shell {
      width: min(100% - 1.25rem, 1080px);
    }

    .about-story-hero {
      padding: 4rem 0;
    }

    .about-story-hero h1 {
      font-size: clamp(3rem, 14vw, 4.4rem);
    }

    .about-story-photo img {
      height: 430px;
    }

    .about-story-section {
      padding: 4rem 0;
    }

    .about-place {
      grid-template-columns: 1fr;
      gap: 0.8rem;
    }

    .about-story-final {
      padding: 4.5rem 0;
    }
  }
</style>

```{=html}
<div class="about-story-page">

  <section class="about-story-hero">
    <div class="about-story-shell about-story-hero__inner">

      <div>

        <p class="about-story-kicker">
          A LITTLE ABOUT ME
        </p>

        <h1>
          I was born curious.
        </h1>

        <p class="about-story-lead">
          This lab is a space where I can share my own journey towards fulfilling
          one of the deepest things I care about: the restoration of our ecosystems
          and the preservation of our local communities. Let me share a little bit
          about myself, on what I do and what my values are. I hope that by reading this,
          you also feel inspired to contribute to your own goals and dreams.
        </p>

      </div>

      <figure class="about-story-photo">

        <img
          src="/images/home/matteo-in-guatemala.jpeg"
          alt="Matteo Arellano travelling by boat with a community group on a river in Guatemala"
        />

        <figcaption>
          Getting to know the river systems in Guatemala. Experiences like this taught me
          that listening is often the first form of useful work.
        </figcaption>

      </figure>

    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="about-story-section about-story-section--white">
    <div class="about-story-shell about-story-narrow">

      <h2>
        I was born in Morelos, Mexico
      </h2>

      <p class="about-story-opening">
        I was born in Mexico, in the state of Morelos, near the center of the
        country. Since I was small, I have always tried to make sense of the
        world around me.
      </p>

      <p>
        Curiosity never really left me. I asked questions, watched how people
        behaved and tried to understand why some systems helped people while
        others seemed to leave them behind.
      </p>

      <p>
        Morelos is a place where history does not feel distant. It remains
        present in the land, language, agriculture, food, music, family life and
        community celebrations. Nahua and Tlahuica histories are part of the
        region, while Náhuatl and traditions such as the Chinelos continue to
        connect people with memory and place.
      </p>

      <p>
        Growing up there taught me that the world cannot be understood only
        through statistics or institutions. Some knowledge lives in stories,
        language, observation, work and the experience passed from one
        generation to the next.
      </p>

    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="about-story-section about-story-section--green">
    <div class="about-story-shell">

      <div class="about-story-narrow">

        <h2>
          The people who shaped how I see the world
        </h2>

        <p>
          I grew up close to two different cultural inheritances. They did not
          cancel one another out. Together, they taught me that identity can
          contain different histories and different ways of seeing.
        </p>

      </div>

      <div class="about-family-grid">

        <article class="about-family-card">

          <span class="about-family-card__label">
            MY GRANDFATHER
          </span>

          <h3>
            Community, memory and Indigenous roots
          </h3>

          <p>
            Through my grandfather and my family’s Indigenous roots, I learned
            to value the relationship between people, land, responsibility and
            community. I learned that knowledge does not belong only to
            universities or books. It is also held in practice, memory and the
            experiences shared between generations.
          </p>

        </article>

        <article class="about-family-card">

          <span class="about-family-card__label">
            MY GRANDMOTHER
          </span>

          <h3>
            History, Greek culture and a wider world
          </h3>

          <p>
            My grandmother introduced me to another cultural inheritance.
            Through her, I became curious about Greek culture and European
            history, including their questions about ethics, society, knowledge
            and the responsibilities people have toward one another.
          </p>

        </article>

      </div>

    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="about-story-section about-story-section--white">
    <div class="about-story-shell about-story-narrow">

      <h2>
        What travelling taught me
      </h2>

      <p>
        After graduating in Rotterdam, my world became much larger. I studied,
        worked and built friendships with people from many countries. Being
        surrounded by so many backgrounds changed how I saw both opportunity
        and responsibility.
      </p>

      <p>
        I began to understand that service, dignity and opportunity are not
        abstract concepts. They look different in every place, and they must be
        understood through the realities of the people living there.
      </p>

      <blockquote class="about-story-quote">
        The more of the world I saw, the more I understood that useful work
        begins by listening.
      </blockquote>

      <p>
        I was also able to participate in projects connected to NGOs,
        entrepreneurship, conservation and local communities. These experiences
        stayed with me because they placed me outside the comfort of theory.
      </p>

      <div class="about-places">

        <article class="about-place">

          <div class="about-place__name">
            ROTTERDAM
          </div>

          <div>

            <h3>
              A global perspective
            </h3>

            <p>
              Studying and beginning my career in Rotterdam placed me among
              people from across the world. Through Rotterdam100, I also
              experienced entrepreneurship as a way to approach social and
              economic challenges, rather than simply as a way to create a
              company.
            </p>

          </div>

        </article>

        <article class="about-place">

          <div class="about-place__name">
            GUATEMALA
          </div>

          <div>

            <h3>
              Teaching, listening and exchanging knowledge
            </h3>

            <p>
              Working alongside communities in Guatemala reminded me that
              meaningful exchange is never one-directional. I arrived prepared
              to contribute, but I also left having learned from people whose
              understanding of their own land and communities was deeper than
              anything I could have brought from outside.
            </p>

          </div>

        </article>

        <article class="about-place">

          <div class="about-place__name">
            COSTA RICA
          </div>

          <div>

            <h3>
              Conservation made tangible
            </h3>

            <p>
              Serving at a sea-turtle shelter brought environmental work close
              to everyday reality. Protection was not an abstract discussion.
              It was physical, patient and shared work.
            </p>

          </div>

        </article>

        <article class="about-place">

          <div class="about-place__name">
            PERU
          </div>

          <div>

            <h3>
              Understanding what can be lost
            </h3>

            <p>
              Being close to work connected to forest protection made the
              consequences of environmental loss more real to me. It also
              reinforced how important it is to listen to the communities that
              have protected and depended on those environments for
              generations.
            </p>

          </div>

        </article>

      </div>

    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="about-story-section about-story-section--sand">
    <div class="about-story-shell about-story-narrow">

      <h2>
        Science and community belong together
      </h2>

      <p>
        I respect scientific thinking deeply. Evidence, transparency,
        reproducibility and the willingness to change direction are essential
        to responsible work.
      </p>

      <p>
        But I also believe science becomes stronger when it remains connected
        to the people and places represented by the data. Communities carry
        knowledge that cannot always be reduced to a spreadsheet.
      </p>

      <p>
        We are living through a period of polarization, environmental pressure,
        economic uncertainty and distrust. Data and technology should not be
        used only to optimize numbers. They should help us understand reality
        more honestly and respond to it more humanely.
      </p>

      <p>
        The same belief influences how I think about business. Healthy
        businesses can create employment, expand opportunity, share knowledge
        and build environments where people can grow. Economic success and
        positive impact should not automatically be treated as opposites.
      </p>

    </div>
  </section>

  <section class="about-story-final">
    <div class="about-story-shell about-story-final__inner">

      <h2>
        Why I built Matteo’s Lab
      </h2>

      <p>
        This Lab is where these different parts of my life come together:
        research, data, technology, business, communities and the responsibility
        to create something useful.
      </p>

      <p>
        It is a place for notes, questions, experiments, visualizations, tools,
        mistakes and discoveries. I do not want it to become a collection of
        polished claims. I want it to remain a living record of how I learn,
        change direction and build.
      </p>

      <p>
        The question behind the Lab is simple:
      </p>

      <blockquote class="about-story-quote-light">
        How can I use my talents in research, data, technology and communication
        to serve people and contribute to work that matters?
      </blockquote>

      <p>
        I do not have every answer. But I can listen, learn and decide what kind
        of work I contribute.
      </p>

      <p>
        My guiding principle remains:
        <strong>Be a Positive Force for Change.</strong>
      </p>

    </div>
  </section>

</div>
```
